title: Delicious Ukrainian Onion Stew
description: A winey, juicy, and comforting Ukrainian dish featuring yellow onion and miso marinade, perfect for a cold winter night, and is plant-based-friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together miso paste, red wine, and vegetable broth to make the marinade.
Add the sliced onions to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the marinated onions to the saucepan and cook for 10 minutes until they start to caramelize.
Add the dried thyme, bay leaves, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the stew for 20 minutes until the onions are tender and the liquid has thickened.
Remove the bay leaves and serve the stew hot in mason jars.
Let the stew cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing.
Reheat the stew to 165 degrees Fahrenheit before serving.
